meta_title: For Students: Antigravity project template to Solve can't scale projects (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome can't scale projects. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Antigravity Project Template: Scale Your AI Apps Fast
content.seo_description: Stop struggling to scale. Learn how to use the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to build professional-grade AI applications as a student.
content.answer_block: The Antigravity template solves scaling bottlenecks by enforcing modular architecture and automated dependency management. By integrating it with Claude Code, you transform from a struggling coder into an AI-augmented architect, allowing you to deploy production-ready applications that handle complexity without the typical technical debt that stalls student projects.
content.intro:
You’ve been there: the initial 'hello world' works, but the moment you add a database or an API layer, your codebase turns into a labyrinth of spaghetti code. The dream of a scalable SaaS app feels like it’s slipping away, buried under the weight of unmanaged state and broken dependencies. You aren't failing; you're just missing the architectural blueprint that industry professionals use to keep their logic clean.

Enter the Antigravity project template. This isn't just boilerplate; it's an opinionated, high-velocity framework designed to handle the heavy lifting. Paired with the raw power of Claude Code, you can now ship features in hours that used to take weeks, turning your portfolio from a collection of scripts into a legitimate, scalable software product.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Scaffold
steps.description: Establish a clean, modular file structure that separates business logic from infrastructure, ensuring your project remains readable as it grows.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a new production-ready project structure using the Antigravity pattern, emphasizing strict separation of concerns between API routes, service layers, and data models.

steps.title: Configure the Context Window
steps.description: Set up your environment variables and context providers to ensure Claude Code understands your project's architectural constraints.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current directory and generate a .claudeignore file and a project context manifest that highlights the core service modules.

steps.title: Build the Service Layer
steps.description: Abstract your AI logic into reusable services, preventing your UI code from becoming bloated and unmanageable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a service layer for handling LLM interactions, ensuring all responses are validated against a Zod schema.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate service layer for AI interactions with schema validation'

steps.title: Implement Automated Testing
steps.description: Ensure your scaling efforts don't break existing functionality by integrating automated regression tests.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate unit tests for the current service layer using the Antigravity testing utility, covering edge cases for API failures.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate unit tests for all new modules'

steps.title: Deploy with Infrastructure as Code
steps.description: Use the built-in deployment patterns to push your project to a staging environment with zero manual configuration.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a deployment configuration for Vercel/AWS that aligns with the Antigravity production standards.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate infra configuration for production deployment'

pro_tips: Always define your Zod schemas before writing the service logic; it forces you to think about data shape first.
Use Claude Code's 'review' command before every commit to catch architectural drift against the Antigravity standard.
Keep your service modules under 200 lines of code; if they grow larger, it's a signal to split them into sub-services.
Leverage the Antigravity 'mock' factory for local development to avoid hitting LLM rate limits during testing.
Treat your prompts as documentation; save them in a /prompts folder to maintain consistency across your project's lifetime.
content.next_steps: Once your project is live, focus on adding a 'Feedback Loop' feature where users can rate AI outputs, which provides the data needed to fine-tune your app's performance.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting scaling issues define your limits. You now have the blueprint, the tools, and the professional mindset to build software that lasts. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
